report_name: AryStinger Botnet Hijacks Thousands of End-of-Life D-Link Routers Using 13-Year-Old Flaws

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The AryStinger botnet spreads by scanning the internet for vulnerable D-Link devices and exploiting security flaws that were first disclosed 13 years ago.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts
attack_patterns.description: The AryStinger payload establishes persistence on compromised routers, ensuring it reconnects to the command-and-control server even after a reboot.

attack_patterns.name: External Proxy
attack_patterns.description: The compromised devices are used as a distributed external proxy network to anonymize the attackers' traffic and launch other malicious campaigns.

malware.name: AryStinger
malware.description: A botnet that compromises end-of-life D-Link routers (such as DIR-850L and DIR-818LW) and NAS devices by exploiting decade-old vulnerabilities. The compromised devices are then used as a distributed proxy network.

identities.name: D-Link
identities.description: Manufacturer of the end-of-life DIR-850L and DIR-818LW routers targeted by the AryStinger botnet.

infrastructure.name: AryStinger C2 Server
infrastructure.description: Command and control server that the AryStinger bots connect to in order to receive commands for proxying traffic.

infrastructure.name: AryStinger Proxy Network
infrastructure.description: A distributed network of at least 4,300 compromised D-Link routers and NAS devices used to proxy malicious traffic.
